title: Child asthma flare with spacer technique and written action plan

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How old is Noah and what has his breathing been like?

transcript.speaker: Parent
transcript.text: He is six. He has been wheezy since yesterday and needed salbutamol every three hours overnight.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any blue lips, exhaustion, trouble speaking, chest recession, fever, or hospital admissions before?

transcript.speaker: Parent
transcript.text: No blue lips or exhaustion. He can talk. Mild fever. He was in hospital once two years ago.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: His oxygen level is 96%, mild wheeze, no recession. Technique with the spacer needs correction.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Give salbutamol 6 puffs via spacer every 4 hours today, then as per action plan. Start prednisolone 30 mg daily for 3 days. Review tomorrow. ED now if reliever needed more often than every 3 hours, difficulty speaking, blue lips, exhaustion, or worsening work of breathing.

candidate_notes.note: 6yo asthma flare, wheeze since yesterday, salbutamol every 3h overnight. Mild fever. Past hospital admission 2 years ago. No cyanosis, exhaustion, speech difficulty, or recession. SpO2 96%, mild wheeze. Spacer technique corrected. Plan: salbutamol 6 puffs via spacer q4h today then action plan; prednisolone 30 mg daily x3 days; written action plan; review tomorrow. ED if reliever needed <3h, difficulty speaking, blue lips, exhaustion, or increased work of breathing.
Child with mild asthma flare. Oxygen normal and speaking normally. Continue salbutamol as needed and return if worse.

S: 6yo wheezy since yesterday, salbutamol every 3h overnight. Mild fever. Previous admission 2 years ago. No cyanosis/exhaustion/speech difficulty. O: SpO2 96%, mild wheeze, no recession; spacer technique poor. A: Moderate asthma flare. P: Salbutamol via spacer and prednisolone. Review tomorrow.

Paediatric asthma flare. Reliever q3h overnight, previous admission, SpO2 96%, mild wheeze, no recession/speech difficulty/cyanosis/exhaustion. Corrected spacer technique. Salbutamol 6 puffs via spacer q4h today then action plan. Prednisolone 30 mg daily x3 days. Written action plan and review tomorrow. ED for reliever need <3h, difficulty speaking, blue lips, exhaustion, or worsening work of breathing.

CDSS asthma pathway: moderate flare with reliever q3h overnight and previous hospitalisation but no severe signs now. SpO2 96%, mild wheeze, no recession. Spacer technique corrected. Treatment: salbutamol 6 puffs spacer q4h today, prednisolone 30 mg daily x3 days, written action plan. Review tomorrow; ED red flags documented.
